reason: The statement applies because the text describes an incident where Dinah, the daughter of Jacob, was defiled by Shechem, a prince of the Hivites. In response, Dinah's brothers, Simeon and Levi, deceived Shechem and Hamor into agreeing to circumcise all the males in their city as a condition for intermarriage. After the men of the city were circumcised, Simeon and Levi took advantage of their weakened state and attacked, killing all the males in the city. This can be seen as a form of infiltration and deception during a conflict.
